meaning: n.培养的器具,孵卵器,早产儿保育器
examples: The incubator provides some interesting projects that might come to fruition in the near future.
Keep this in mind as you read about the incubator components in this section—they are very cool but incomplete.
The nation-state, though he was well aware of its parochial and xenophobic limitations, seemed to be liberty's best incubator.
In standard IVF, embryos are removed from the incubator once a day to be checked under the microscope.
The incubator also links the entrepreneurs up with office space for 12-weeks, mentors, and possible investors.
